bullets.text: Built multi-modal ML model integrating genomic, proteomic, and clinical data (n=12,000) to predict treatment response, achieving AUC 0.87 in held-out validation.
Led cross-functional team of 4 data scientists and 2 bioinformaticians; owned quarterly roadmap and presented findings to C-suite and clinical advisory board.
Deployed SHAP-based explainability layer on top of XGBoost pipeline, enabling clinicians to understand model drivers and increasing adoption by 60%.
Designed and ran HPC workflows on SLURM clusters (400-node) to process 50TB of sequencing data, reducing runtime from 6 days to 14 hours.
Applied GROMACS and AMBER MD simulations to characterize protein-ligand binding for 3 oncology targets; findings contributed to 2 peer-reviewed publications.
Developed QSAR model (random forest + RDKit fingerprints) for membrane permeability prediction with F1 of 0.91, integrated into internal screening pipeline.
Collaborated with AstraZeneca's oncology team as external partner to evaluate computational hits against CDK4/6 targets.
Built time-series churn model (LightGBM) on 2M customer records, reducing quarterly churn by 12% through targeted retention campaigns.
Designed ETL pipeline in Airflow + dbt pulling from 6 data sources into a unified feature store used by 3 downstream models.
LightGBM ensemble on 9 molecular fingerprint types (ECFP4, MACCS, RDKit) to predict active compounds from high-throughput screens.
Scaffold-based GroupKFold cross-validation to prevent data leakage from chemical similarity; Tanimoto diversity reranking of top candidates.
Nextflow DSL2 pipeline integrating WGS, RNA-seq, and MS-proteomics data with automated QC, normalization, and differential analysis steps.
MOFA+ factor analysis to identify shared latent structure across omics layers in a 500-patient cancer cohort.

details: Thesis: Data-driven approaches for protein-ligand binding prediction

achievements.text: Top performer, CrossTALK ML Competition (UofT DSI, 2024) — predictive hit retrieval from high-throughput drug screens.
Co-inventor, US Patent 11,XXX,XXX — macrocyclic peptide inhibitor design targeting oncogenic eIF4E.
Published ML model for peptide membrane permeability (Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling, 2021, F1=0.91).
